strategic_profile.executive_summary: Texas Tech University is a large, public, Carnegie R1 research institution located in Lubbock, Texas. Known for strong programs in engineering, agriculture, and business, it offers a traditional, spirited campus life with a holistic admissions philosophy for students who do not meet initial assured admission criteria.
strategic_profile.market_position: Flagship Public Research University / Hispanic-Serving Institution (HSI)
strategic_profile.admissions_philosophy: Holistic for non-assured admits; Assured admission based on class rank and test scores

analyst_takeaways.insight: Engineering and Nursing are significantly more competitive than other majors, with Engineering admitting around 55% of applicants compared to the overall 67% rate.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants to the Whitacre College of Engineering should prepare for higher academic scrutiny and possess strong math/science fundamentals.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Introductory Biology and First-Year Architecture function as heavily enforced weed-out courses, with strict grading curves.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Pre-med and architecture students must employ rigorous time management immediately upon enrollment to survive initial attrition rates.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The university is widely recognized for its high-spirited athletic culture, vibrant Greek life (15% participation), and strong rural/agricultural ties.
analyst_takeaways.implication: The environment strongly favors students seeking a traditional, socially active large-state-school experience, though the remote location requires adjustment.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Vibrant and highly social, heavily centered around Big 12 athletics, Greek life, and over 450 student organizations. Described affectionately by some students as a 'drinking town with a college problem,' prioritizing a balanced approach to social and academic life is essential.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Located in a relatively isolated region of West Texas. While the campus itself is large but navigable, students often rely on cars to travel outside Lubbock. A commuter-friendly campus for upperclassmen.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: As a Carnegie Tier One (R1) institution, undergraduate research is accessible, particularly in agriculture, wind energy, and biosciences, with faculty open to taking on driven undergraduates.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-optional for admissions and most merit scholarships. Students lacking test scores or not meeting assured admission thresholds are evaluated holistically.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Texas Tech University does not publicly utilize an admissions waitlist.

gpa.notes: Average admitted unweighted GPA is approximately 3.56.

testing.policy_note: 59% of enrolled students submitted SAT scores and 40% submitted ACT scores.

colleges.name: Edward E. Whitacre Jr. College of Engineering
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students with strong standardized test scores and rigorous high school math/science backgrounds. Transfer and non-assured admits enter the eXplore STEM program.
colleges.housing_profile: Traditional residence halls with some Living Learning Communities (LLCs) for engineering students.
colleges.student_archetype: Driven, STEM-focused, capable of handling intense early coursework and strict grading curves.

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.special_requirements: Internal transfers must have 12 hours completed at TTU, a 3.0 GPA, and a C or better in Calculus I.
majors.notes: Students who do not meet the initial 3.0 admission standard are placed in eXplore STEM until prerequisites are met.

colleges.name: Jerry S. Rawls College of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students seeking comprehensive business training with strong networking and corporate placement opportunities.
colleges.student_archetype: Career-oriented, highly social, and active in extracurricular networking.

colleges.name: College of Arts & Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for pre-health, humanities, and general science students.
colleges.student_archetype: Pre-med hopefuls, life-science researchers, and liberal arts students.

majors.name: Biology
majors.notes: BIOL 1403 and 1404 are heavily known as pre-med weed-out courses with large lecture sizes and stringent grading.

colleges.name: Huckabee College of Architecture
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Dedicated, hard-working students prepared for intense studio hours and high attrition rates.
colleges.student_archetype: Creative, highly dedicated students willing to endure long nights in the studio.

majors.name: Architecture
majors.notes: First-year architecture is explicitly designed to weed out students; only a small percentage advance to the second year.

application_deadlines.notes: Priority deadline for fall freshman admissions and scholarship consideration.

supplemental_requirements.details: Requires a separate Honors College Application and specific ACT/SAT thresholds (1360+ SAT or 30+ ACT) along with 3 letters of evaluation for the pre-med track.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Legacy status and alumni relations are expressly ignored in the holistic admissions review process at Texas Tech, creating a purely merit and context-driven evaluation.

major_selection_tactics: Apply to non-impacted majors (e.g., General Studies) if your stats fall below the 50th percentile, as programs like Nursing and Engineering are significantly more selective.
If interested in restricted majors but lacking the assured admission criteria, expect to be placed in the 'eXplore' program to complete foundational coursework before transferring.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Applicants rejected from their restricted first-choice major (like Engineering or Business) are automatically placed into the eXplore STEM or University Undeclared program. Students can then internally transfer after maintaining a 2.75 - 3.0 TTU GPA and passing early prerequisite courses.

financials.tuition_model: Per credit hour with flat-rate options; differential tuition charged per college (e.g., Engineering charges an extra $90/SCH).
financials.aid_philosophy: Merit-focused, does not meet 100% of demonstrated need

scholarships.name: Presidential Merit Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Awarded to top 10% graduates with 1400+ SAT. Non-residents receiving this scholarship qualify for in-state tuition waivers.

scholarships.name: Proven Achievers Scholarship for Transfers
scholarships.benefits: Qualifies out-of-state transfer students for an in-state tuition waiver.

scholarships.name: IB Honors Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Provides an extra scholarship to entering First-year students who complete the IB Diploma.

ap_policy.general_rule: Texas Tech broadly accepts AP scores of 3 or higher for college credit in introductory courses.
exceptions: Biology requires a score of 5 to receive credit for BIOL 1403/1404.
AP World History requires a 3 to earn credit for HIST 2322.
AP Art History requires a 3 to earn credit for ARTH 2302.
ap_policy.usage: Credits can bypass heavy weed-out courses and satisfy foundational core requirements.

ib_policy.general_rule: Grants credit for IB exams; requirements vary by department.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Transfer credits must be from accredited institutions; non-traditional or vocational credits are generally not accepted.

what_it_takes: Strong time-management skills to balance intense academics with a highly active social and Greek life.
Mental resilience to push through heavily curved, large-lecture weed-out courses in the first year.
common_activities: Attending Big 12 football and basketball games
red_flags: Over-indulging in the 'party culture' during freshman year, which frequently leads to academic probation or failing out of STEM programs.
Failing to utilize office hours and TA review sessions for notoriously difficult foundational courses like Intro Biology and Chemistry.
insights: Lubbock's geographic isolation means the university heavily drives the town's culture. Students must be proactive in finding community, as the physical scale of the campus can be isolating initially.
Students placed into the eXplore program should not view it as a rejection, but must treat their first-year GPA with extreme seriousness to declare into their desired restricted major.
